# Architecture
<!-- Generated by handover v1.2.1 -->

## Tech Stack

- **Language:** Python 3.12+
- **CLI framework:** Typer or Click
- **Data contracts:** Pydantic v2
- **Storage:** SQLite with FTS5 (full-text search)
- **Parsing:** Tree-sitter Python bindings with per-language grammars
- **File watching:** watchdog (event-driven, not polling)
- **MCP server:** local stdio transport
- **Testing:** pytest
- **Logging:** structured logging (JSON-compatible)

## Monorepo Layout

```
context-router/
├── apps/
│   ├── cli/            # Typer CLI entrypoint
│   └── mcp-server/     # MCP server entrypoint
├── packages/
│   ├── contracts/      # Shared Pydantic schemas and interfaces ONLY
│   ├── core/           # Orchestration, use-case coordinators, plugin loader
│   ├── graph-index/    # File scanner, symbol graph, dependency edges, test mapping
│   ├── memory/         # Observations, decisions, stale-memory detection
│   ├── runtime/        # Stack trace, test failure, log, lint parsers
│   ├── ranking/        # Mode-specific rankers, token budget, explain output
│   ├── language-python/
│   ├── language-java/
│   ├── language-dotnet/
│   ├── language-yaml/
│   ├── adapters-claude/
│   ├── adapters-copilot/
│   ├── adapters-codex/
│   ├── storage-sqlite/ # Repository pattern over SQLite, migrations
│   ├── workspace/      # Multi-repo registry, cross-repo link model
│   └── benchmark/      # Harness, baselines, task suite, report generation
└── docs/
    ├── architecture.md
    ├── roadmap.md
    └── adr/
```

## System Design Notes

- All modules communicate through typed contracts defined in `packages/contracts`. No module imports implementation details from another module.
- Language analyzers are plugins that implement the `LanguageAnalyzer` interface and return normalized `Symbol` and `DependencyEdge` objects — never raw Tree-sitter nodes.
- Storage is accessed only through repository interfaces in `storage-sqlite`. Rankers and analyzers never touch SQLite directly.
- Runtime parsers publish `RuntimeSignal` objects to an internal event bus consumed by the ranking pipeline.
- The MCP server and CLI are thin shells over `core` use-case coordinators. No business logic lives in either app layer.
- File-system updates use watchdog event-driven callbacks, not polling loops.
- Optional LLM-assisted summarization is always behind a capability flag and never on the critical path.
